summary: Mobile-first (≤768px) UX pass: safe-area padding, 44px-class touch targets on key marketing and learner chrome, learner bottom-nav pathway truncation, context bar height, and settings drawer close control. No routing, SEO URLs, or entitlement logic changed.
routesImproved: Learner app shell (/app/*) when GlobalContextBar or bottom primary nav is visible
issuesAddressed: Marketing mobile header: notch safe-area inset on brand row; guest Login/Sign up min height 44px
Marketing signed-in mobile CTA row: primary actions use min-h-11
Secondary header actions (Dashboard-style links): min-h 38px → 44px where HEADER_SECONDARY_ACTION_CLASS is used
Learner GlobalContextBar: row and pills taller on small screens for touch
MobileContextDrawer: larger close hit target; section labels 10px → 11px
Learner bottom nav: pathway pill truncate + title tooltip; slightly wider grid gap
Learner shell: bottom padding includes safe-area-inset-bottom for home indicator above fixed bottom nav

beforeAfterNotes.before: Guest marketing CTAs used 36px min-height; mobile header ignored safe-area-top; context pills sat in a 36px-tall row; pathway label could crowd five-column bottom nav; learner content could sit tight against iOS home indicator.
beforeAfterNotes.after: Guest CTAs and key secondary actions align to ~44px minimum; header respects safe-area-top; context bar and drawer close control are easier to tap; pathway pill truncates with full label on long-press/hover via title; extra bottom inset clears the home gesture area.

remainingLimitations: Automated screenshot/visual regression not added — manual check at 320–414px still recommended for lesson detail, question session, and flashcard surfaces.
Individual page-level tables/filters were not exhaustively restyled; use overflow-x-auto patterns where wide tables appear.
Marketing mega-menu desktop row unchanged (lg+); mobile continues to use hamburger accordion pattern.
typecheckNote: Full-package tsc may report pre-existing errors in unrelated tests/libs; touched layout files pass ESLint with no new issues.
